subject: concept:meme-ecosystem-competition
quote: In the real world, memes do not exist in isolation and their infection and decay parameters would be functions of other memes already present. This makes the parameter values time-dependent, which is not accounted for in this model.
rationale: Paper explicitly names meme-meme parameter coupling — a meme's infection/decay rates depend on competing memes — as a limitation, the exact ecosystem-competition idea.

subject: concept:meme-ecosystem-competition
quote: To properly model the infection and decay parameters, the entire memetic ecosystem must be taken into account.
rationale: Paper states fitness is not intrinsic: infection/decay parameters must be modeled relative to the whole competing meme population. Direct evidence for the concept.

subject: concept:meme-ecosystem-competition
quote: The inability of the model to handle multiple peaks is a result of some of the simplifying assumptions made in constructing the model. Besides the constant N, the infection and decay parameters α, β and γ are treated as independent constants.
rationale: Paper diagnoses its own single-peak failure as caused by treating meme parameters as independent of competing memes — motivates ecosystem-competition modeling.

subject: concept:replicator-ontology
quote: As defined by Dawkins, a meme is any cultural entity that appears to exhibit self-replication. In particular, this concept applies to human thoughts and ideas, which can be transmitted from person to person via social interaction.
rationale: Paper adopts the discrete self-replicating-unit definition of a meme wholesale from Dawkins as its load-bearing premise — the replicator-ontology assumption.

subject: concept:replicator-ontology
quote: Since the SIR model was a basis for this work, the success of the modelling indicates that memes can indeed be considered to be infectious in nature
rationale: Paper treats memes as discrete countable infectious units (people are S/I/R w.r.t. one meme), operationalizing the replicator ontology in a formal model.

_note: Strongest hit: meme-ecosystem-competition — the paper's discussion section explicitly flags meme-meme parameter coupling as the cause of its single-peak failure and as required future work, matching the catalog gap note. replicator-ontology is assumed uncritically (no critique of discrete units). The other five concepts are absent: the paper uses a fully-connected homogeneous-mixing model and explicitly disclaims network structure ('every individual in I has an equal effect on every individual in S'), so there are NO superspreader/high-degree dynamics — it is the anti-superspreader model. No algorithm, no epistemic-vigilance/receiver scrutiny, no spread-vs-endorsement gap (search volume = interest only), and no Yule/random-copying neutral-drift baseline (its null is a deterministic SIR ODE, not a stochastic neutral model).
